question: Explain the significance of Rajeev Motwani's work in the development of the PageRank algorithm.
expectedPoints: Motwani was the PhD advisor to Larry Page and Sergey Brin at Stanford.
He provided crucial theoretical guidance and probabilistic analysis that underpinned the early PageRank concept.
His expertise in randomized algorithms and data mining was instrumental in transforming the idea into a practical model for web search.

question: Describe Rajeev Motwani's key contribution to the field of streaming algorithms.
expectedPoints: He co-authored a highly influential paper that formalized the 'streaming model' of computation.
The model deals with processing massive data streams under severe constraints of memory (sublinear space) and time (single pass).
This work laid the foundation for efficient algorithms to approximate frequency moments and other statistics on huge, rapidly arriving datasets.

question: Beyond his research, name one major contribution Rajeev Motwani made to the theoretical computer science community and briefly describe its impact.
expectedPoints: He was a prolific mentor and advisor, guiding numerous students who became leaders in academia and industry (e.g., the Google founders).
He co-founded the 'SIGACT Innovations in Theoretical Computer Science' conference, a premier venue for cutting-edge research.
He authored a highly regarded textbook, 'Randomized Algorithms', which educated a generation of students and researchers.
